name: What if faulty equipment from a subcontractor hurt me—can I sue them?

mainEntity.name: What if faulty equipment from a subcontractor hurt me—can I sue them?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. You can collect workers' comp from your employer AND file a third-party personal injury lawsuit against the negligent subcontractor or equipment manufacturer for additional damages.
acceptedAnswer.description: If a subcontractor’s faulty equipment or a manufacturer’s defective tool caused your injury on a Utah site, you likely have two separate legal paths. First, you file a workers' compensation claim through your own employer for immediate medical and wage benefits. Second, you can file a 'third-party liability' lawsuit against the subcontractor or manufacturer. This is crucial because workers' comp does not pay for 'pain and suffering,' but a third-party lawsuit does. These cases often involve complex 'indemnity' contracts between various companies on the site. Identifying every potential defendant is the key to full financial recovery. A lawyer's role is to manage both claims simultaneously without letting one jeopardize the other.
